column1: # DOLCHEO+ encoding TSV — schema per canon/governance/writing-conventions.md

column2: Surface proxy cold-start as a distinct race lane (cold vs cached)
column3: The film demo race runs three lanes at once — RAW (no transform) / 1ST HIT (cache MISS: cold transform + transfer) / CACHED (cache HIT: transfer only). Cold-start is the proxy one-time transform cost on a cache miss; it is server-side, so it is ADDED to the cold lane and NOT scaled by the network bandwidth slider. Measured live once per session via a HEAD cache-HIT vs forced-MISS (per-session cache-buster) timing delta, isolating transform cost from RTT, with a measured ~1.2s fallback.
column4: Live curl probes: cold transform ~1.0-1.4s vs warm ~90ms. HEAD returns content-length + X-Transcode-Encode-W/H same-origin (no worker change). Hardcoded byte matrix matches live proxy exactly. Implemented formula simulated: cache wins 25-38x everywhere; cold first-hit degrades as bandwidth rises to a ~1.1x near-tie with raw at 5G — surfaced, not hidden. bun test 60/60 incl. page guards; typecheck clean.
column5: Two-pass cold-then-warm on one lane; warm-only + first-request toggle; bake cold-start as a constant (rejected: not self-truthing)

column4: Three-phone race layout unverified at mobile widths
column5: raceSc/lanePush per-viewport + verdict-below-trio offset are best-effort, not eyeballed. Faithful visual check requires serving via the worker (wrangler dev/deploy), not file://, since the live cold-start probe and header reads are same-origin. Tuning knobs centralized in layoutFor().
